page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.u0.c1m1.xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the ecs.u0 server family. It features 4 dedicated physical CPU cores with 4 vCPUs, operating with a single thread per core configuration. The instance is provisioned with 4.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a memory-to-core ratio of 1.0 GB per core. It runs on the KVM hypervisor.

This instance does not include bundled local storage or integrated GPU hardware. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. There are zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this configuration.

From a resource density perspective, the ecs.u0.c1m1.xlarge offers a low memory-to-CPU ratio of 1:1. This hardware profile represents a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring dedicated CPU execution without large memory footprints. Suitable workloads include lightweight compute tasks, basic web serving, utility scripting, and small-scale distributed processing nodes that do not require local storage or high memory capacity.

With zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses, this instance is designed for workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation with minimal memory requirements. The low memory-to-CPU ratio makes it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for lightweight compute tasks, basic web serving, and utility processing nodes that do not depend on local storage or high memory capacity.

tagline: An entry-level dedicated x86_64 compute instance featuring four vCPUs, four gigabytes of memory, and baseline network bandwidth of two gigabits per second.
bullet_points: Dedicated x86_64 architecture with 4 physical cores and 4 vCPUs
4.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:1 memory-to-CPU ratio
KVM hypervisor execution with 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
No bundled local storage or integrated GPU accelerators
Designed for lightweight compute tasks and basic web serving
